a:r.a:t: When the coordinator agent decomposes a complex task, it creates a structured task plan that specifies exactly what each worker should do and how they should pass results back. Each task specification includes an executor - the worker agent type like rag_worker, data_analyst, utility, general, graph_manager, or verifier. Tasks can run in sequential or parallel execution modes. Terminal states are completed, failed, stopped, or waiting_message (when a worker needs clarification from the coordinator). The 8 artifact handoff types define the structured data that workers pass back to the coordinator and finalizer. These include title_candidates for document identification, doc_focus for narrowing to specific documents, research_facets for breaking research into angles, facet_matches for results mapped to research angles, doc_digest for document summaries, subsystem_inventory for architecture inventories, policy_guidance_matches for compliance findings, and buyer_recommendation_table for comparative analysis. Document ranking uses 6 keys to prioritize which documents matter most: is_meta_document flags high-level overview docs, reviewed_relevance scores human-verified importance, matched_facets counts how many research angles a doc covers, strong_evidence_count tallies high-confidence citations, title_path_score measures filename relevance, and seed_hits counts initial search matches. This structured approach ensures worker outputs are machine-parseable and the finalizer can synthesize coherently.
